mapping_type_legend.direct: NORS code clearly and specifically maps to this authority citation.
mapping_type_legend.topic_based: NORS code and authority share the same topic area; not a single-section exact match.
mapping_type_legend.related: Authority is relevant context but is not the primary citation for this complaint type.
mapping_type_legend.uncertain: Mapping is plausible but not confirmed by official crosswalk materials; treat as a suggestion.
mapping_type_legend.state_overlay: Connecticut-specific statute or regulation that supplements federal authority.

records.resident_right_concept: Freedom from physical abuse

federal_regulations.title: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Duties of the representatives of the Office

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut DPH Nursing Home State Regulations

records.notes: Use A01 when the physical force is intentional. Use A05 (Gross neglect) for repeated care failures resulting in injury. Perpetrator codes apply (01=staff, 02=resident, 03=family/rep, 99=other).

records.resident_right_concept: Freedom from sexual abuse

federal_regulations.title: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Duties of the representatives of the Office

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut DPH Nursing Home State Regulations

records.notes: Use D03 for non-sexual disrespect. A02 covers both contact and non-contact sexual acts.

records.resident_right_concept: Freedom from psychological abuse

federal_regulations.title: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation

records.notes: Use D03 for less severe staff rudeness or insensitivity. A03 is for willful psychological mistreatment.

records.resident_right_concept: Protection from financial exploitation

federal_regulations.title: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation

records.notes: Use E02 for facility mismanagement of property/trust funds. A04 is for intentional exploitation for another's benefit.

records.resident_right_concept: Freedom from gross neglect; essential care

federal_regulations.title: Freedom from abuse, neglect, and exploitation

records.notes: Use F or J codes when gross neglect is not clearly indicated. A05 requires a serious risk to health/safety.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to access medical, financial, and administrative records

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to Access/Purchase Copies of Records

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut General Assembly Nursing Home Statutes

records.resident_right_concept: Right to information in understandable language/format

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to Forms of Communication with Privacy

records.resident_right_concept: Right to access Ombudsman program without interference

federal_regulations.title: Establishment of the Office of the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut Statutes — Office of the Long Term Care Ombudsman

records.notes: Use B01 when resident is having difficulty accessing records (not ombudsman access specifically).

records.resident_right_concept: Voluntary admission; non-discriminatory admissions practices

appendix_pp_tags.title: Equal Practices Regardless of Payment Source

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut General Assembly Nursing Home Statutes

records.resident_right_concept: Right to appeal transfer/discharge and insurance decisions
records.notes: Use K02, K03, or K04 if the appeal issue is with Medicaid, managed care, or Medicare (not the facility).

records.resident_right_concept: Right to remain; discharge/transfer notice and due process

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut General Assembly Nursing Home Statutes

records.notes: 30-day notice required under 42 CFR §483.15(c)(4). F622-F625 were reorganized into F627/F628 in November 2024 Appendix PP revision.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to be notified of room or roommate changes
Right to choose own physician, pharmacy, hospice, and care provider

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to Choose/Be Informed of Attending Physician

records.notes: Use L02 if resident is permitted to choose but the chosen provider is unavailable.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to transition planning and return to community

appendix_pp_tags.title: Provision of Medically Related Social Services

keywords: not referred to local contact agency
records.notes: Use L03 when barriers are not related to facility action.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to be treated with respect and dignity

appendix_pp_tags.title: Respect, Dignity/Right to have Personal Property

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut General Assembly Nursing Home Statutes

records.notes: Use A03 for psychological abuse. D03 is for less severe rudeness/insensitivity.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to privacy in care and personal affairs

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to Forms of Communication with Privacy

records.notes: Use B03 if lack of privacy is related to Ombudsman visits.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to file and have grievances addressed

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut General Assembly Nursing Home Statutes

records.resident_right_concept: Right to be free from retaliation for exercising rights

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut Statutes — Office of the Long Term Care Ombudsman

keywords: call light not answered after complaining
records.notes: Use F02 if the call light issue is not retaliatory. May add additional codes if resident is abused or neglected as a result.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to choose visitors and when to visit
Right to form and participate in resident and family councils
Other civil rights and personal liberty
records.notes: Catch-all for rights not covered by D01-D08. Use D04 for privacy-specific complaints.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to accurate billing and no improper charges
Right to retain and have facility protect personal property

appendix_pp_tags.title: Respect, Dignity/Right to have Personal Property

records.notes: Use A04 if facility is misusing resident funds for benefit of others.

records.resident_right_concept: Freedom from accident hazards; adequate supervision
records.notes: Use A05 if this is a repeated problem that facility staff fail to address.

records.resident_right_concept: Timely response to requests for assistance
records.notes: Use D06 if slow response is in reaction to a resident complaint or advocacy action.

records.resident_right_concept: Person-centered care plan with resident participation

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to Participate in Planning Care

keywords: not included in care plan meeting
PASRR services not in care plan
not given copy of care plan

records.resident_right_concept: Safe and accurate medication administration
Right to hygiene and personal care services
records.notes: Use A05 if there is failure to meet essential care needs.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to timely healthcare services
records.notes: Use D01 if denied choice of medical provider.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to identification and treatment of symptoms
records.notes: Use A05 if failure to treat threatens health/safety/welfare. Use F580 when notification of changes is the issue.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to incontinence and catheter care
Right to assistive devices and adaptive equipment
Right to specialized rehabilitative services
Right to be free from physical restraints

appendix_pp_tags.title: Right to be Free from Chemical Restraints
Free from Unnecessary Psychotropic Meds/PRN Use

appendix_pp_tags.title: Activities Meet Interest/Needs of Each Resident

records.nors_major_label: Activities, Community Integration and Social Services
records.resident_right_concept: Right to transportation assistance
records.notes: No direct F-tag maps to this NORS code. Topic-based link to 42 CFR §483.10 self-determination provisions. Use L02 if transportation is from an outside provider not arranged by facility.

records.nors_major_label: Activities, Community Integration and Social Services
records.resident_right_concept: Ombudsman assistance with resident-to-resident conflicts

records.notes: G03 is unique in being complaint-type that specifically centers Ombudsman mediation. Use L01 for resident conflict with representative/family.

appendix_pp_tags.title: Provided Diet Meets Needs of Each Resident

keywords: adaptive equipment not provided for meals
records.notes: Use A01 or A05 for willful food deprivation.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to therapeutic and culturally/religiously appropriate diet

appendix_pp_tags.title: Provided Diet Meets Needs of Each Resident

records.resident_right_concept: Right to safe, comfortable physical environment
Right to safe, maintained building
Right to adequate supplies and furnishings
records.notes: Use H01 if shortage is of food. Use E02 for lost personal property.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to accessible building and grounds
Right to clean environment and pest-free facility
records.notes: Use E02 for lost laundry.

records.resident_right_concept: Facility management accountability and compliance

connecticut_overlay.title: Connecticut DPH Nursing Home State Regulations

records.resident_right_concept: Facility financial stability; adequate resources for resident care
records.notes: Use I03 if shortage of supplies is not related to fiscal management.

records.resident_right_concept: Right to adequate and competent staff

connecticut_overlay.title: AN ACT CONCERNING NURSING HOME STAFFING LEVELS

records.notes: Use J01 if the administrator is absent or on site infrequently. CT PA 19-89 adds state posting requirement.

records.resident_right_concept: Access to effective regulatory oversight

federal_regulations.title: Functions and responsibilities of the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman

records.notes: Complaint is against the regulatory agency, not the facility. No direct F-tag; 45 CFR §1324.13(a)(7) grants ombudsman systems advocacy authority on this.

records.resident_right_concept: Medicaid eligibility and coverage rights
records.notes: Complaint is against Medicaid/DSS, not the facility. No CFR 483 F-tag applies. Ombudsman may assist per 45 CFR §1324.13.

records.resident_right_concept: Managed care coverage and appeals rights
records.notes: Complaint is against managed care plan. No CFR 483 F-tag applies.

records.resident_right_concept: Medicare eligibility and coverage rights
records.notes: Complaint is against Medicare/CMS, not facility. Observation status is a significant issue.

records.resident_right_concept: VA eligibility and coverage rights
Private insurance coverage rights
Resident self-determination against family/representative interference

federal_regulations.title: Duties of the representatives of the Office

records.notes: Use A01-A05 (with perpetrator code 03) if family/representative rises to the level of abuse, neglect, or exploitation. No Appendix PP/F-tag mapping is assigned because L01 is a non-facility representative/family conflict code unless facility action is also involved.

records.resident_right_concept: Access to independently arranged outside services
records.notes: Use F06 if the facility failed to arrange services it was responsible for. L02 is for services independently arranged by resident/family.

records.nors_minor_label: Request to transition to community setting
records.resident_right_concept: Right to transition assistance from non-facility sources

federal_regulations.title: Functions and responsibilities of the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman

records.notes: Use D02 when the facility itself is the barrier to transition. L03 is for non-facility system barriers.
